entries.en: You have something on your shirt.

entries.ko: 그러니까 우리가 지금 가도 된다는 거야?

entries.ko: 그럼 우리가 지금 가도 된다는 뜻이야?

entries.ko: 아니야 별거 아니야, 그냥 놀라서 그래.
entries.en: Oh, it's nothing. It's just that I was surprised.

entries.ko: 저기 버스 정류장에 앉아 있는 저 사람.
entries.en: The guy over there sitting at the bus stop.
The one next to him with glasses.
Why don't you go talk to him?
How did the camping trip go?
It was way better than I expected.
How are you getting along with the new nanny?
Have you gotten your vaccine appointment set up?

It's just one of those things.

entries.ko: 그 얘기가 나온 김에 하나 물어봐도 돼?
entries.en: While we're on the subject, can I ask you something?

entries.ko: 그 사람이 그런 걸 할 수 있을까?
entries.en: Is she up to something like that?

I could really use a cup of coffee.

entries.ko: 너 좀 쉬어야 할 것 같은데.
entries.en: You look like you could use a break.

entries.ko: 그 사람 아들 얘기였던 것 같아.

entries.en: Something about the signal being weak.

entries.ko: 그거 (원래 있던 곳으로) 가져다 놓아야 해.

entries.en: It's just going to be the two of them.

entries.ko: 할 수 있는 데에는 한계가 있어.
entries.en: There's only so much you can do.

entries.ko: 한 번에 할 수 있는 일은 정해져 있어.
entries.en: I can only do so many things at a time.

entries.ko: 너 스스로 할 수 있는 것엔 한계가 있어.
entries.en: You can only do so much on your own.

entries.ko: 몸이 감당할 수 있는 스트레스엔 한계가 있어.

entries.ko: 그래서 나는 혼자 사는 게 좋아.
